verses.2: I will open my mouth in a parable.
verses.3: which we have heard and known,
verses.4: We will not hide them from their children,
verses.5: For he established a covenant in Jacob,
verses.6: that the generation to come might know, even the children who should be born;
verses.7: that they might set their hope in God,
verses.8: and might not be as their fathers—
verses.9: The children of Ephraim, being armed and carrying bows,
verses.12: He did marvelous things in the sight of their fathers,
verses.13: He split the sea, and caused them to pass through.
verses.14: In the daytime he also led them with a cloud,
verses.15: He split rocks in the wilderness,
verses.16: He brought streams also out of the rock,
verses.17: Yet they still went on to sin against him,
verses.18: They tempted God in their heart
verses.20: Behold, he struck the rock, so that waters gushed out,
verses.21: Therefore the LORD heard, and was angry.
verses.22: because they didn’t believe in God,
verses.23: Yet he commanded the skies above,
verses.24: He rained down manna on them to eat,
verses.25: Man ate the bread of angels.
verses.26: He caused the east wind to blow in the sky.
verses.27: He also rained meat on them as the dust,
verses.28: He let them fall in the middle of their camp,
verses.29: So they ate, and were well filled.
verses.30: They didn’t turn from their cravings.
verses.31: when the anger of God went up against them,
verses.32: For all this they still sinned,
verses.33: Therefore he consumed their days in vanity,
verses.34: When he killed them, then they inquired after him.
verses.35: They remembered that God was their rock,
verses.36: But they flattered him with their mouth,
verses.37: For their heart was not right with him,
verses.38: But he, being merciful, forgave iniquity, and didn’t destroy them.
verses.39: He remembered that they were but flesh,
verses.40: How often they rebelled against him in the wilderness,
verses.41: They turned again and tempted God,
verses.43: how he set his signs in Egypt,
verses.44: he turned their rivers into blood,
verses.45: He sent among them swarms of flies, which devoured them;
verses.46: He also gave their increase to the caterpillar,
verses.47: He destroyed their vines with hail,
verses.48: He also gave over their livestock to the hail,
verses.49: He threw on them the fierceness of his anger,
verses.50: He made a path for his anger.
verses.51: and struck all the firstborn in Egypt,
verses.52: But he led out his own people like sheep,
verses.53: He led them safely, so that they weren’t afraid,
verses.54: He brought them to the border of his sanctuary,
verses.55: He also drove out the nations before them,
verses.56: Yet they tempted and rebelled against the Most High God,
verses.57: but turned back, and dealt treacherously like their fathers.
verses.58: For they provoked him to anger with their high places,
verses.59: When God heard this, he was angry,
verses.60: so that he abandoned the tent of Shiloh,
verses.61: and delivered his strength into captivity,
verses.62: He also gave his people over to the sword,
verses.64: Their priests fell by the sword,
verses.65: Then the Lord awakened as one out of sleep,
verses.67: Moreover he rejected the tent of Joseph,
verses.68: But chose the tribe of Judah,
verses.69: He built his sanctuary like the heights,
verses.70: He also chose David his servant,
verses.71: from following the ewes that have their young,
verses.72: So he was their shepherd according to the integrity of his heart,
